what_it_does: Defines a hand-rolled external store that holds backend-derived registry data and transient gesture/hook handles so components inside tldraw can read Hud/backend state via useSyncExternalStore or imperative getters.
observations: Replaces six module-scope mutable globals with one external store and a subscribe/snapshot API.
State fields are OINFO, MODEL_OPTIONS, UI_INFO, NODE_STATES, and COGNITION_INFO.
Also exports module-scope mutable refs CONNECT, DRAG_CONN, and FORCE_RUN with setter functions.
set() performs a shallow merge and emits all listeners.
Imperative getters are provided for non-React call sites.
Heavy use of Record<string, any> for registry payloads.
Explicitly avoids zustand/redux because node_modules is symlinked to a pinned commit.
Comments are tagged with short codes like F0, F3, C1, L-fe, D4.
standouts: The store is intentionally dependency-free and hand-rolled.
Node state rendering is driven by design-token names and shape names from the backend.
COGNITION_INFO is treated as a sibling projection to OINFO/UI_INFO.
conventions: Follows a reflects-never-owns policy: the store mirrors backend truth, never becomes a source of truth.
Exports both a React external-store interface and imperative accessors.
Comments tie each section to design-doc codes and rules.
concerns: Module-scope mutable variables (CONNECT, DRAG_CONN, FORCE_RUN, state) can be mutated from any importer.
set() emits on every call even if the resulting state is unchanged.
Default CONNECT and FORCE_RUN are no-ops, so calling them before installation silently does nothing.
DRAG_CONN lives outside the store snapshot, so React subscribers do not see gesture changes.
Broad any types weaken type safety for registry contents.
notes: This file is the bridge that lets tldraw-internal shapes read backend registry state without relying on Hud-level React context.
questions: Which component or controller calls setConnect/setForceRun and when?
How is the store consumed inside the tldraw shape—via useSyncExternalStore directly?
What fetches populate OINFO, UI_INFO, MODEL_OPTIONS, NODE_STATES, and COGNITION_INFO?
Is COGNITION_INFO.node_states expected to share the same schema as NODE_STATES?
How is DRAG_CONN cleaned up if a drag is canceled?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it consolidates scattered globals into one external store that mirrors backend truth for shape consumption.
apparent_themes: external store bridging React context boundaries
intention_signals: The file is trying to give deeply nested tldraw shape components reactive, read-only access to backend registries and controller hooks while keeping all authoritative state on the backend.
novelty: It encodes node status rendering as backend-issued design tokens (token/shape) and extends the same idea to a cognition projection, so engine-side registration drives canvas paint without frontend edits.
contribution: A dependency-free external store plus typed node-state render definitions that expose backend registry snapshots to both React and imperative callers, and provide installable hook handles for connect and force-rerun actions.
summary_for_embedding: A hand-rolled external store module that holds backend-derived registry state (OINFO, MODEL_OPTIONS, UI_INFO, NODE_STATES, COGNITION_INFO) and transient gesture/hook handles for consumption by tldraw-internal shapes and non-React callers.
intention_for_embedding: To replace scattered module globals with a single React-readable external store that mirrors backend truth for canvas shapes and controllers.

symbols.does: Declares the shape of a node-state render descriptor.
symbols.description: Defines NodeStateRender as an object carrying a design-token name (token), an optional icon, and an optional shape, used by the backend to tell the frontend how to paint a node state.
Defines NodeStateDef with id, label, optional means/applies_to/derived_when, and an optional render descriptor; this is the type the shape reads to paint status labels and tokens.

symbols.does: Declares the full store state type.
symbols.description: Defines RegistryState as the snapshot shape containing OINFO, MODEL_OPTIONS, UI_INFO, NODE_STATES, and COGNITION_INFO, all read-truth mirrors of backend registries.
Exports setConnect, which assigns the module-scope CONNECT function used by shapes to request a backend connection on pointer-up.
Exports setDragConn, which assigns the module-scope DRAG_CONN object shared across shape instances during a live drag gesture.
Exports setForceRun, which assigns the module-scope FORCE_RUN function the controller uses to bypass a node's memo gate.
Defines emit, which iterates the listener set and invokes each callback when the store snapshot changes.
Exports getOINFO, an imperative accessor returning the live OINFO record for non-React callers such as graph loading and edge logic.
Exports getMODEL_OPTIONS, an imperative accessor returning the live model-id registry for non-React callers.
Exports getUI_INFO, an imperative accessor returning the live UI-component registry used to validate ui:// targets.
Exports getNODE_STATES, an imperative accessor returning the live node-states index so the shape can read labels and render tokens.
Exports getCOGNITION_INFO, an imperative accessor returning the live cognition projection so the cognition view can look up role/node render tokens.
